title: Causal neutralization-discount proxy
summary: Safety Car and VSC periods lower racing speed and can make pit stops strategically attractive, but there is no explicit safety-car column.
rationale: Safety Car and VSC periods lower racing speed and can make pit stops strategically attractive, but there is no explicit safety-car column. Formula 1 sources define SC/VSC as reduced-speed race states; these should leave signatures in field-wide lap-time inflation, compressed gaps, and clustered pit stops.
implementation_hint: For each Race_Year/LapNumber, compute leave-one-driver-out field median LapTime_Delta, lap-time dispersion, number/share of cars pitting, and position-change volatility. Feed only lagged versions from lap t and t-1 to predict PitNextLap at t, plus interactions with NeedsAnotherStop and LapsRemaining_Est.
expected_effect: Should help identify opportunistic stops that static compound-life windows miss, especially wet or incident-heavy races.
risk: Same-lap or future-lap aggregate features can leak if not lagged carefully. In pre-season testing, slow laps may mimic neutralization but have different meaning.
